creator: mi+mill@aldan.algebra.com
text:
=D0=CF=CE=C5=C4=A6=CC=CF=CB 19 =CC=C9=D3=D4=CF=D0=C1=C4 2007 09:01 =C4=CF, =
bf =F7=C9 =CE=C1=D0=C9=D3=C1=CC=C9:
> This allows the port to be built with lang/gcc42,
> although not with the patched system gcc 3.4.6 (I'm
> not expecting you to support the latter, though -- I
> just mention it for your information).

The port builds the library using the system's bsd.lib.mk -- just as the=20
system libraries are built.

It declares the WARNS-level of 3 expecting the system to construct sensible=
=20
set of compiler warning flags.

Thus it is possible, that unusual compilers will still generate warnings --=
=20
they would also break the system build, unless NO_WERROR is set.

Yours,

 -mi
